Trustee, Pacifica School District
1562 Miranda Ct.
Pacifica, CA 94044
Your Reqr-rest for Advice
Our File No. A-08-097
Dear Mr. O'Neill:
Re
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Please note that our advice is confined to the
Act and we, therefore, offer no advice on other laws that coulcl be applicable, such as
Elections Code Section 18680.
QUESTIONS
May a committee that was primarily formed to sllpport a school parcel tax
nleasure on the June 3, 2008, ballot retain funds it has after the election to sLlpport
an anticipated future school parcel tax measure for the same school district?
If the committee is not allowed to retain its surplus funds to support a measure in
the future, may the funds be donated to the school clistrict or to the Education
Foundation, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization established under federal law?
CONCLUSIONS
I ' Yes, a ballot measLlre committee may retain funds left over from the election to
sllpport an anticipated ballot measllre for the same pllrpose in a future election.
2. Since question one is answered in the positive, an answer to this qlrestion is
unnecessary.
I The Political Retirrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections lll000 throuch 91014. All

Political Practices commission are eontained in Sections lgl l0 thr.ou-gh 1g997 of ritle 2 of the Califirrnia
Clode tlf Regulations. All re-uulatory rc'l'erences are to Title 2. Division 6 of rhe Calilirrnia Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
2

Page No.2
FACTS
You are the Trustee of the Pacifica School District located in San Mateo County.
on the June 3, 2008, ballot, a school parcel tax measure, known as Measure N, was
defeated' since it did not receive the required two-thirds of the uor.rr: ,.-,fpln.' n."o,,r.
it was a close contest, yollr committee would like to retain left over funds io fto."
another school parcel tax measllre on the ballot for voter consideration.
rhere is nothing in-the o.,*u,l)::;::r,r 
a primar,y rormed barot measl,re
committee from retaining funds it holds on the day of the election. However, if the
committee makes expenditures to support or oppose state or local candidates or any other
California committee, including other ballot miasure committees, it may qualify as a
different type of committee. This would require the committee to amend its Statement of
organization (Form 410) within 10 days of qualifying as a different type of committee.
(Section 84103.) If the committee is used to-suppt.tit" qualification ano passage of a
future ballot measure only, the new measur.'r uiphu designation, when it is available
from the local Registrar of Voters, must be included in th"e amendment of the Form 410.
(Section 84102(d).)
For a review of the reporting requirements of a ballot measure committee whose
measure failed, please review Campaign Disclosure Manual 3. we have included a copy
of Cctmpaign Disclosure Marunl 3; Informationfor Committees primarily Formed to
Support or oppose a Ballot Meosure with this leiter for your convenience. We also note
that there are provisions in the Elections Code goveming the use of funds raised in
connection with ballot measure elections. Although the-Commission does not interpret or
enforce these provisions, they are provided in Apfendix I of the campaign manral.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
